214,PullRequestEvent,2016-10-17 17:19:04+00:00,4576441,kbandla/dpkt,8379186,andreamerello,,,89665696,,Radiotap extpresent,open,False,306,"My atheros dongle dongle actually pushes frames with extended present flags. This requires the parser to, at least, skip the extra present flags in order to continue parsing from the right place.

Incidentally this arose another problem: the radiotap header specifications requires each field to be aligned to the start of the packet, and when there are two present flags bitmap, this is even less obvious. In particular the TSFT ended up to be improperly aligned here.
This patch makes the radiotap parser to take care of both there issues, and fixes wrong shift/mask for extended present flag bit.

Since my editor is kind enough to auto-remove trailing spaces, another trivial patch for this shows up also here..",https://api.github.com/repos/kbandla/dpkt/issues/306,,,2016-10-17T17:19:04Z,2,30,8,2,[],[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/kbandla/dpkt/pull/306.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/kbandla/dpkt/pulls/306/commits,380,"['3d071a5c5efecbd5ca7d0d88b19850372ead6a5d', '180d24bf614d970c2936b34e0726a00c01c65b6f']"

318,PullRequestEvent,2017-05-14 15:52:23+00:00,21590545,wtolson/gnsq,544858,wtolson,,,120429788,,Updates to allow use with nsq v1.0.0-compat,closed,False,13,"The latest release of nsq finally deprecates the changes that were started in 0.2.29, removing `<action>_<object> ` urls on nsqd and nsqlookupd in favor of `<object>/<action`.   It also changes the way data is returned in json, using the top level of the json object, rather than the `data` attribute within the top level object.
These are the necessary changes to support the 1.0.0-compat release.

I've removed the pre 0.2.29 part of the testing matrix, since those won't work with the new changed http interfaces.   The old interfaces have been deprecated for almost 3 years and it'd be possible to support these, but the code would be substantially more convoluted.
There's also a bit of setup.py hackery, as the latest versions of gevent no longer support python 2.6
column2: it needs to explicitly install gevent 1.1.

column1: There's no other functionality changes, and the only testing changes made were those necessary to support the version tag '1.0.0-compat', which choked the integration_server.py tests.",https://api.github.com/repos/wtolson/gnsq/issues/13,2017-05-14T15:52:22Z,2017-05-14T15:52:22Z,2017-05-14T15:52:23Z,3,31,19,6,[],[],[],[],wtolson,544858.0,User,False,[],https://github.com/wtolson/gnsq/pull/13.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/wtolson/gnsq/pulls/13/commits,380,"['f42ea71ad475729465878173302be5607e961063', '2fce0aad5c58c12750fcf7d92206df50ef190ba3', 'dcc2883faf975be221efb66d33ac20788b6cf3d1']"

column1: >This patch improves the quality of strategies inferred from Numpy dtypes:
>* Integer dtypes generated examples with the upper half of their (non-sign) bits
>  set to zero.  The inferred strategies can now produce any representable integer.
>* Fixed-width unicode- and byte-string dtypes now cap the internal example
>  length, which should improve example and shrink quality.
>* Numpy arrays can only store fixed-size strings internally, and allow shorter
>  strings by right-padding them with null bytes.  Inferred string strategies
>  no longer generate such values, as they can never be retrieved from an array.
>  This improves shrinking performance by skipping useless values.
>This has already been useful in Hypothesis - we found an overflow bug in our
>Pandas support, and as a result :func:`~hypothesis.extra.pandas.indexes` and
>This release fixes a performance problem in tests where
>Tests experience a slow-down proportionate to the amount of code they cover.
>This is still the case, but the factor is now low enough that it should be
>unnoticeable. Previously it was large and became much larger in 3.28.4.
>:func:`~hypothesis.strategies.from_type` failed with a very confusing error
>are now unwrapped correctly, and strategy inference works as expected.
>This release makes some small optimisations to our use of coverage that should
>reduce constant per-example overhead. This is probably only noticeable on
>examples where the test itself is quite fast. On no-op tests that don&#39
>anything you may see up to a fourfold speed increase (which is still
>significantly slower than without coverage). On more realistic tests the speed
>up is likely to be less than that.
>This release fixes some formatting and small typos/grammar issues in the
>documentation, specifically the page docs/settings.rst, and the inline docs
>This release improves the handling of deadlines so that they act better with
>1. The deadline is raised during the initial generation and shrinking, and then
>   lowered to the set value for final replay. This restricts our attention to
>   examples which exceed the deadline by a more significant margin, which
>2. When despite the above a test still becomes flaky because it is
>   significantly faster on rerun than it was on its first run, the error
>   message is now more explicit about the nature of this problem, and includes
>   both the initial test run time and the new test run time.
>In addition, this release also clarifies the documentation of the deadline
>setting slightly to be more explicit about where it applies.
>This release blocks installation of Hypothesis on Python 3.3, which
>:PEP:`reached its end of life date on 2017-09-29 &lt
>This should not be of interest to anyone but downstream maintainers -
>if you are affected, migrate to a secure version of Python as soon as
>possible or at least seek commercial support.

418,PullRequestEvent,2017-09-16 20:20:28+00:00,19384977,peplin/pygatt,184212,peplin,,,115687955,,bgapi discovery improvements,closed,False,121,"This pull request primarily addresses #118. On some setups it is necessary to wait for a short moment after restarting the USB adapter and or retry a few times before it can be talked to again. Otherwise the `_open_serial_port` call will fail and abort with ""No BGAPI compatible device detected"".
* Wait half a second after restarting the USB adapter
* Revert 9ac2bf9 to not abort after the first connection attempt anymore. Even with a delay after restarting it may still fail otherwise.
* Add the actual observed exception to the ""No BGAPI compatible device detected"" error print so that the user can tell what happened
* Clarify some of the connection establishment debug prints to make it more clear what is happening.

* Use the bgapi backend. See for example [the minimal setup in the README](https://github.com/peplin/pygatt#example-use). On an affected setup the `adapter.start()` will fail without the changes in this branch.
* Run the example without an adapter connected to see an  exception notice printed in addition to the hardcoded error message.

Note that I am not too familiar with pygatt beyond my debugging. So I have not run any other tests other than using this branch on my setup.",https://api.github.com/repos/peplin/pygatt/issues/121,,2017-09-16T20:20:28Z,2017-09-16T20:20:28Z,7,10,9,1,CONTRIBUTOR,[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/peplin/pygatt/pull/121.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/peplin/pygatt/pulls/121/commits,380,"['da49cf162f36639972889aaad3887a84c0eeb5a2', '3f0f5393036afbfef684befba86256cfd9bdee3c', '6ef0a5175c70b261ca5069f17b888374ce227d4c', 'f3128a63f18e7c9f49d5fe8574fdb4d8d6b16cc9', '3433ee99ce38f21baf77c3d214dc382eaf2006c0', '80cc93fbcda4b1e21fbefed465088ad5924b1369', '00281568b1ea49c34025c38caa3268ae647b9963']"

624,PullRequestEvent,2017-11-21 15:36:37+00:00,1408162,jpadilla/pyjwt,339286,jquense,,,153923707,,X5c jwk support,open,False,309,"Adds a small amount for support for certificates around jwk's along with small conveniences I'd like to see.
- the RSA algo now accepts a cert or a key in `to_jwk`
- adds the `x5c` and `x5t` keys to the jwk
- `from_jwk` will accept a dict and skip json deserializing if so (our jwks are already in memory)
- `from_jwk` uses the cert to return the public key if available.

So i decided to go through and redo the ctypes binding a bit. Things should be a lot less fragile when using it standalone now.

There are still a couple of thing i need to do. As it stands if you try to use an older version of freetype you will most likely get an error on import because of it unable to find a newer function. I want to get that fixed before this is merged.

Eventually I'd like to go through and fill out more of the freetype api than what is defined so far just so things are more complete. For that i went and put comments of the names that freetype gives its headers. Then for headers that already had some functions defined from it i went and put a comment with the names of functions not yet defined.
Also for the binding function i could use the restype and argtypes method, but i just generally prefer to do it the way i did in the code if I'm writing a function that does the stuff.

Fixed issue whereby python2 `str` (same as `bytes`) objects were written as property list&#39
Added `use_builtin_types` option to `plistlib.load/dump` to control whether `&lt
column3: ` elements should be loaded as built-in `bytes` (this is the default when running ufoLib under python3), or whether they should be wrapped in a `plistlib.Data` object (default when running ufoLib under python2)
column4: when used with the `dump` function, this option controls whether `bytes` values should be written as `&lt

column1: - The `lxml` and `singledispatch` requirements are now optional. When not present, the built-in `xml.etree.ElementTree` library is used. To install ufoLib with lxml support you can do like this: `pip install ufoLib[lxml]`.
- Added new `ufoLib.plistlib` implementation that works the same way on both python 2 and python 3, and matches the python3&#39
- Added new `ufoLib.etree` module that exports the same ElementTree interface using either `lxml` or `xml.etree` (built-in) as backends, depending on the availability of former (161).
- glifLib: use double quotes in XML declaration like previous ufoLib versions (159).
- Set up test coverage and upload coverage report to codecov.io.
Fixed issue when parsing GLIF xml from a Unicode string containing an explicit XML encoding declaration. The latter is ignored and UTF-8 encoding is always assumed (157).
- Fixed issue introduced in v2.2 whereby the XML declaration was not written at the top of GLIF files and in the string returned from `writeGlyphToString` function (157).
- Make validation on the default for read and write (prior it was off for write)
- Fixes issue whereby the required lxml dependency was not correctly specified in the setup.py&#39
- Added options to turn validation off on read and write. This can be globally, or per bit of data. For UFOWriter, validation is on by default. For UFOReader, validation is off by default.
- Switched to lxml from ElementTree. lxml is now a required dependency.
- When converting to UFO3, ufoLib will now remove the older mmk group prefixes
- When calculating area, auto close pen (this is somewhat controversial, and may change in the future)
- Add validation support (see validation options in UFOLib)
- No longer stores representations in objects without a dispatcher.
- Added kerning.find() method, to find a value for a pair, even if the pair is not specifically defined in the kerning
column2: i.e., the method is given a glyph name that has kerning, but only because it is in a kerning group.

column1: - When saving a new font created from scratch, or one loaded from a different path, to the location of another existing UFO, the latter is automatically overwritten with the new one (180).
- When saving a font in-place and the existing UFO is invalid, overwrite the latter (165, 180).
- Allow to rename the default layer when saving in-place (165, 180).
Fixed issue with MathKerning not copying groups when processing multiplication or division, resulting in kerning exceptions getting lost (110, 111, ufoProcessor6).

2187,PullRequestEvent,2015-01-01 01:57:57+00:00,24561828,gunthercox/ChatterBot,2030578,gunthercox,,,26739863,,Introduce database for storing conversation records,open,False,18,"Until this point chatterbot has used flat files to store statements from conversations. This pull request implements database storage which will allow several useful changes to be implemented in order to improve results.
- [x] The database setup allow all possible responses for a known statement to be queried.
- [x] The number of times for which statements occur is now being recorded. This is used to weight response results such that more common responses are chosen.

- [ ] Profiles have been created and are are used to track statements said by each user. This will be an element that can be used in the future to gauge potential sarcasm and also to return more relevant responses by making them user-specific.

- [ ] Testing has been rebuilt to account for these changes",https://api.github.com/repos/gunthercox/ChatterBot/issues/18,,,2015-01-01T01:57:56Z,1,253,359,6,[],[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/gunthercox/ChatterBot/pull/18.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/gunthercox/ChatterBot/pulls/18/commits,380,['18c40a95d752a1d1e272c404fd6b9fc98bae933e']

I had a use case where i need to get the latest response of the same message. For instance, for the text 'hi' there are two responses 'hello' and 'hey' in the database. What i want is, I need to get the response which has been included recently (say 'hey' in this example).

I checked out the response_selection  method and it seems to have get_first_response and other kind of methods but this one is not included. So i created a new method called get_recent_response in chatterbot.response_selection.py file and it seems to fulfill my use case.
I thought may be we can add this to our chatterbot module. So this is the reason why i created this pull request.  Please checkout the code and if any queries feel free to ask.

2419,PullRequestEvent,2016-06-06 07:44:23+00:00,6544500,peterjc/backports.lzma,63959,peterjc,,,21301809,,Declare namespace to setuptools if it is installed.,closed,False,9,"I think this should address the issues from #8.
It is all confusing for a few reasons:
- The setup.py from ``backports.ssl_match_hostnamez`` uses setuptools while the setup.py from ``backports.lzma`` uses distutils.

- The README from the [original backports module](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/backports) suggests that you should use a particular template in the ``backports/__init__.py`` file in reference to a PEP... but that pep [was rejected](http://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0382/).
- That pep suggests that setuptools provides its own roughly equivalent workaround, one which of course only works with setuptools.

So, in 0f36997 I try first to use the setuptools workaround (which seems to be necessary if setuptools is installed?) and if it is absent, then fall back to the phrase put forward in [pep-0382](http://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0382).",https://api.github.com/repos/peterjc/backports.lzma/issues/9,,2016-06-06T07:44:23Z,2016-06-06T07:44:23Z,1,8,5,1,[],[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/peterjc/backports.lzma/pull/9.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/peterjc/backports.lzma/pulls/9/commits,380,['0f3699732d8f20bfaec82c0a4b6cb30b1407034c']

But as far as I could tell the proposed alteration would have destroyed the ""remember-ability"" of the password by randomly mutating the case at the character level.
Also it was mentioned that this sort of thing might be best handled in a post processing step, but this

>A number of incompatible changes were made in this release, with the intent of removing features deprecated for a long
>time or change existing behaviors in order to make them less surprising/more useful.
>* Reinterpretation mode has now been removed.  Only plain and rewrite
>  mode are available, consequently the ``--assert=reinterp`` option is
>  no longer available.  This also means files imported from plugins or
>  ``conftest.py`` will not benefit from improved assertions by
>  explicitly turn on assertion rewriting for those files.  Thanks
>* The following deprecated commandline options were removed:
>* ImportErrors in plugins now are a fatal error instead of issuing a
>  pytest warning (`1479`_). Thanks to `The-Compiler`_ for the PR.
>* Removed support code for Python 3 versions &lt
>* Removed all ``py.test-X*`` entry points. The versioned, suffixed entry points
>  were never documented and a leftover from a pre-virtualenv era. These entry
>  points also created broken entry points in wheels, so removing them also
>  removes a source of confusion for users (`1632`_).
>* ``pytest.skip()`` now raises an error when used to decorate a test function,
>  as opposed to its original intent (to imperatively skip a test inside a test function). Previously
>  this usage would cause the entire module to be skipped (`607`_).
>* Exit tests if a collection error occurs. A poll indicated most users will hit CTRL-C
>  anyway as soon as they see collection errors, so pytest might as well make that the default behavior (`1421`_).
>  A ``--continue-on-collection-errors`` option has been added to restore the previous behaviour.
>  Thanks `olegpidsadnyi`_ and `omarkohl`_ for the complete PR (`1628`_).
>* Renamed the pytest ``pdb`` module (plugin) into ``debugging`` to avoid clashes with the builtin ``pdb`` module.
>* Raise a helpful failure message when requesting a parametrized fixture at runtime,
>  e.g. with ``request.getfixturevalue``. Previously these parameters were simply
>  never defined, so a fixture decorated like ``pytest.fixture(params=[0, 1, 2])``
>  Thanks to `nikratio`_ for the bug report, `RedBeardCode`_ and `tomviner`_ for the PR.

>  and is just a synonym for ``--maxfail=1``.

>  including unittest-style Classes. If set to ``False``, the test will not be
>* New ``doctest_namespace`` fixture for injecting names into the
>* New ``--doctest-report`` option available to change the output format of diffs
>* New ``name`` argument to ``pytest.fixture`` decorator which allows a custom name
>  for a fixture (to solve the funcarg-shadowing-fixture problem).
>* New ``approx()`` function for easily comparing floating-point numbers in
>* Ability to add global properties in the final xunit output file by accessing

>* New ``pytest_make_parametrize_id(config, val)`` hook which can be used by plugins to provide

3338,PullRequestEvent,2017-04-11 15:59:37+00:00,32090358,ethereum/pyrlp,824194,pipermerriam,6250754.0,ethereum,113979410,,Allow desirealization into a mutable object,closed,False,32,"### What was the problem
All objects returned from either `rlp.decode` or `Serializable.deserialize` are currenty immutable and the `rlp` API has nothing available for making things `mutable`.
Implemented the *inverse* of the `make_immutable` API.  There is now both a standalone `make_immutable` function as well as a `make_immutable` class method on the `Serializable` type.
The current behavior of defaulting to immutable objects on deserialization is preserved.

3351,PullRequestEvent,2018-05-25 05:19:43+00:00,77478378,ethereum/py-evm,286254,glaksmono,6250754.0,ethereum,190484176,,Enable logging to file by default for alpha releases,open,False,777,### What was wrong?

Users of the alpha releases are very likely going to encounter errors for which we need debug level logging output to diagnose. They however are unlikely to be both logging at the `DEBUG` level and retaining log output.
Lets enable file based logging in a `$XDG_TRINITY_ROOT/<chain-dir>/logs` at the `DEBUG` level. This way when a user has an issue we can get them to dig out the relevant logfiles for us.

In order to implement comprehensive tests for https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/pull/552 we needed to add functionality to `eth_tester` that allowed one to query past chain state using the `call` method.

A block_number parameter was passed to the `_execute_and_revert_transaction` function and then also passed through to `_get_normalized_and_signed_evm_transaction` function in order to make sure the nonces matched so that the call method's ""transaction"" could be executed and then reverted.

3987,PullRequestEvent,2018-07-07 18:08:20+00:00,112676803,apple/turicreate,56061,hoytak,10639145.0,apple,199859996,MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MTk5ODU5OTk2,Ensure that current table_printer uses always print the last line of progress,closed,False,821,"I really wanted to do some more cleanup in this file, but it was in danger of becoming a rabbit hole. For now, a quick fix that relies on the fact that all current table_printer uses but one have tracking enabled: have `print_footer()` just print the last tracked row if it wasn't already printed. We just need to add a little additional state so that we know when a `flex_float` actually originated from a `progress_time` or when a `flex_int` actually originated from a `bool`.
Also update the one non-tracking toolkit to print the final progress row explicitly.

4604,PullRequestEvent,2016-09-17 23:44:08+00:00,60379729,quantopian/alphalens,1215939,sketchytechky,1393215.0,quantopian,85691009,,Fixing cases when ValueError from alphalens.tears.create_factor_tear_sheet,open,False,87,"When we run create_factor_tear_sheet on either:
1. single value for factor per group (only one value over a date, or sector)
2. Field with discreet value (not sure if this is a correct use case for alphalens)
We are can run into exception seen below.
`qcut` over similar edges have been discussed but no solution is implemented yet ""officially"":
So this provided a simple work around for case when we run into this issue in a simple case, when we have only seen one value of the factor.
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas/tools/tile.pyc in qcut(x, q, labels, retbins, precision)
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pandas/tools/tile.pyc in _bins_to_cuts(x, bins, right, labels, retbins, precision, name, include_lowest)
--> 189         raise ValueError('Bin edges must be unique: %s' % repr(bins))

4705,PullRequestEvent,2018-02-09 14:08:05+00:00,33653601,jupyter/notebook,327925,takluyver,7388996.0,jupyter,168248445,,Testing with Selenium & Sauce labs,open,False,3321,"This replaces the `js/tree` section of the tests with Selenium tests run with `py.test`, and sets up Travis to run it using browsers on Sauce Labs, which has a [free open source plan](https://saucelabs.com/open-source). The tests can also be run locally with a suitable webdriver installed ([geckodriver](https://github.com/mozilla/geckodriver/) for Firefox, [chromedriver](https://sites.google.com/a/chromium.org/chromedriver/) for Chrome).

My thinking is that this can form the basis for replacing more of the JS tests with Selenium tests. I think this is a nicer way of writing tests, it's more realistic because it drives a real browser rather than Qt webkit, and hopefully it won't suffer so much from random failures caused by async issues.
Sauce has some nice features - you can browse through screenshots at the different steps, to help diagnose problems:

4761,PullRequestEvent,2018-05-30 06:54:12+00:00,83748372,h2oai/datatable,4231472,st-pasha,1402695.0,h2oai,191342653,,Implement proper compilation with AddressSanitizer,closed,False,1064,Running Asan on a Python extension module is challenging. In theory it should be possible, but [the documentation](https://github.com/google/sanitizers/wiki/AddressSanitizerAsDso) states ""Use at your own risk"". In practice, I wasn't successful at running Asan via `LD_PRELOAD` or `DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES` as suggested.

This PR uses a different approach: create a new executable that *embeds* Python (as described [in python docs](https://docs.python.org/3/extending/embedding.html)). This executable however will be compiled with static Asan support. Then I can use that executable as if it was ""real"" python, by installing it into a fresh virtual environment.
Running `datatable` with Asan revealed a single instance of unsafe memory read, which this PR also fixes.

4894,PullRequestEvent,2018-05-14 08:48:45+00:00,93721432,awslabs/sockeye,713105,logogin,3299148.0,awslabs,166235876,,[WIP] draft of float16 dtype support in training/inference,closed,False,287,"This is a first draft of complete training/inference with `float16` support. We can keep discussion and further updates within this thread.
For inference, encoder and decoder may have different dtypes. For encoder `--encoder-dtype <float16|float32>`, and for decoder `--decoder-dtype <float16|float32>` respectively.
* Almost all operations and symbols (except `mx.sym.FullyConnected` and activations) seems to be 2-3 times slower in `float16` than `float32`.
* `mx.sym.batch_dot` has no support for `float16` in upstream mxnet. Patch will be provided by @KellenSunderland at some moment.
* Only `mx.sym.FullyConnected` seems to have proper support for tensor cores. Other symbols that may require matrix multiplication should be *simulated* via FC layer, i.e.`mx.nd.norm`.
* Embeddings should run in `int32`, since `float16` has only integers up to ±2K.
* No unit tests and style checks. Tests may fail during the build.

* `dtype` can be passed between app layers as numpy type or as plain string. Typed object has advantages as it encapsulates more information and internally all string type names get  converted into `numpy.dtype` at some moment. However, string name is easier to store in config files and seems like neither numpy api nor mxnet has problems with accepting type as plain string.

* Transfering `dtype` from call to call as argument pollutes the code. It could be more effective to store it once in a global config object. However encoders and decoders have polymorphic implementations. Should `dtype` be a singleton across all encoders and decoded or every instance can be configured with its *own* `dtype`?
* What should be the contract between training and inference `dtype`? Currently I can suggest next one:
1. During training `dtype` is stored in config file.
2. For inference `dtype` is read from config.
3. User may override `dtype` for inference with cli arguments.

There are `test/translate.py` and `test/train.py` scripts which bootstrap minimal inference and training. Models can be used from [tutorials](https://github.com/awslabs/sockeye/tree/master/tutorials) for seq2seq and WMT data.
Those scripts can be used to debug/profile simple cases. Uncomment/modify necessary parts to adjust for your workspace or use-cases.
To dump parameters from layer to layer, uncomment monitors in `inference.py` line 163,168.
`test/compare_params.py` can be used to plot precision drifts between layers.

5022,PullRequestEvent,2017-03-28 22:23:38+00:00,38581626,pybind/pybind11,1203629,wjakob,17565521.0,pybind,113007280,,Deprecate `object::borrowed`/`object::stolen`,closed,False,771,~~Provides (via a dummy template parameter hack) inline definitions of object::borrowed/object::stolen.~~
The constexpr static instances can cause linking failures if the compiler doesn't optimize away the reference, as reported in #770.

There's no particularly nice way of fixing this in C++11/14: we can't inline definitions to match the declaration aren't permitted for non-templated static variables (C++17 *does* allows ""inline"" on variables, but that obviously doesn't help us.)

One solution that could work around it is to add an extra inherited subclass to `object`'s hierarchy, but that's a bit of a messy solution and was decided against in #771 in favour of just deprecating (and eventually dropping) the constexpr statics.

> Several of our automated tests have show that synchronization and publishing
> tasks are being polled only once and immediately failing with a
> Turns out that we have some tasks triggered by the automation that sit on the
> `planned` state for a while and since `'planned' != 'running'` we get out of
> the loop and stop polling right after the first check.
`_poll_task` aborts polling if a task is anything other than the ""running""
state. This is bad logic, as a task may proceed through the following states:
* Abort polling if a task enters the ""paused"" or ""stopped"" state.
* Continue polling if a task is in any other state, such as ""planning"",

This is a crude method (using exceptions for message passing) that shouldn't change the behaviour of existing codebases using this library. The situation I'm thinking of avoiding right now is an exception raised in a `dehydrate_` method bringing down an entire export. Something like the following in a `Resource` subclass can now avoid this:

I considered a separate `Resource.skip_resource(obj)` method to be called before `export_resource(obj)` but such functionality can be achieved by overriding `export_resource(obj)` anyway, and the latter can handle `dehydrate_` method calls.

I've tacked a `.skipped` attribute on the dataset returned by `Resource.export()`, I have an uneasy feeling about it but this avoids breaking the API while still returning information about what was skipped. 5981,PullRequestEvent,2016-03-09 23:12:49+00:00,33880218,cloudpipe/cloudpickle,836375,rgbkrk,10352495.0,cloudpipe,62310106,,Allow pickling of builtin methods,closed,False,57,"This resolves #56, which found that things like `cloudpickle.dumps(itertools.chain.from_iterable)` and `cloudpickle.dumps(object.__new__)` failed because these builtin methods do not have a `__code__` attribute and `pickle.whichmodule` can't determine their module. The solution here is to intercept these things and send them to `save_reduce`.

I had to use slightly different solutions for Python 2.7 and >=3.4. I couldn't resolve it at all for 3.3: looks like doing so would require getting deep into the details of pickling. In 3.2 and 3.3, trying to unpickle the pickled method results in ""NEWOBJ class argument has NULL tp_new error"". So to avoid people pickling data and then being unable to unpickle it, the current version refuses to pickle these methods for those python versions.",https://api.github.com/repos/cloudpipe/cloudpickle/issues/57,2016-03-09T23:12:48Z,2016-03-09T23:12:48Z,2016-03-09T23:12:48Z,2,32,0,2,[],[],[],[],rgbkrk,836375.0,User,False,[],https://github.com/cloudpipe/cloudpickle/pull/57.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/cloudpipe/cloudpickle/pulls/57/commits,380,"['3ca9d71b188556fded2e112c7e01a34b398a0fba', '62027de3b76552fc2294e04b2e89c186b9270238']"

raise DistutilsPlatformError(""VC %0.1f is not supported by this module"" % VERSION)

6270,PullRequestEvent,2017-09-08 12:58:43+00:00,744916,gem/oq-engine,2463856,micheles,316546.0,gem,139968398,,Saved memory in event_based_risk,closed,False,3004,"Some simplification done 3 months ago when implementing the new gmf_ebrisk calculator caused the engine to use more memory than before. I was aware of the fact, but the impact was minor on the calculations I studied at the time, so I was not worried. However Mabe' calculation is particularly bad since the hazard part is extremely heavy (mostly due to a large maximum_distance and lots of sites), has a big logic tree and the memory effect is particularly severe. With the change implemented in this PR we are back computing the hazard for each GSIM and not for several GSIMs at once. In the case of Mabe' this reduces by 3 the amount of needed memory. Still the computation does not run on the cluster since it requires way more than 2 GB per core.
A serious change of strategy is needed to perform such calculations, but this fix is good enough for the moment. The release of engine 2.6 is not blocked anymore.

The demos with Python 2.7 are running here: https://ci.openquake.org/job/zdevel_oq-engine/2578",https://api.github.com/repos/gem/oq-engine/issues/3004,2017-09-08T12:58:43Z,2017-09-08T12:58:43Z,2017-09-08T12:58:43Z,1,23,11,2,CONTRIBUTOR,[],"[{'login': 'daniviga', 'id': 1818657, 'avatar_url': 'https://avatars0.githubusercontent.com/u/1818657?v=4', 'gravatar_id': '', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga', 'html_url': 'https://github.com/daniviga', 'followers_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/followers', 'following_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/following{/other_user}', 'gists_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/gists{/gist_id}', 'starred_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/starred{/owner}{/repo}', 'subscriptions_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/subscriptions', 'organizations_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/orgs', 'repos_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/repos', 'events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/events{/privacy}', 'received_events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/daniviga/received_events', 'type': 'User', 'site_admin': False}]",[],micheles,2463856.0,User,False,[],https://github.com/gem/oq-engine/pull/3004.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/gem/oq-engine/pulls/3004/commits,380,['766a4e572c38357a996455332f073bf6d6f60144']

6273,PullRequestEvent,2017-10-13 14:02:04+00:00,744916,gem/oq-engine,2463856,micheles,316546.0,gem,146384035,,More refactoring of the risk calculators,closed,False,3127,"The final goal is to make it easier to use the engine as a library for risk calculations. There is still a long
way to go, but with this refactoring the scenario_risk calculator (simplified) can be written in around 40 lines of code:
# extract the sites and the assets from the exposure
# filter the sites with the rupture
L = len(oq.lti)  # number of loss types
# compute average losses for each asset, averaging on the events
# out has shape (L, A, E, I)
mean = out.array[:, a, :, :].mean(axis=1)  # on the events
# compute total by summing on all assets

The API will likely change in the future, but this is an example of what can be done right now, useful for the risk scientists. Warning: I have not tested this example much, but at least it gives the right aggregated losses for the ScenarioRisk demo.",https://api.github.com/repos/gem/oq-engine/issues/3127,2017-10-13T14:02:03Z,2017-10-13T14:02:03Z,2017-10-13T14:02:04Z,13,152,130,18,CONTRIBUTOR,[],"[{'login': 'raoanirudh', 'id': 1707414, 'avatar_url': 'https://avatars1.githubusercontent.com/u/1707414?v=4', 'gravatar_id': '', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh', 'html_url': 'https://github.com/raoanirudh', 'followers_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/followers', 'following_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/following{/other_user}', 'gists_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/gists{/gist_id}', 'starred_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/starred{/owner}{/repo}', 'subscriptions_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/subscriptions', 'organizations_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/orgs', 'repos_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/repos', 'events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/events{/privacy}', 'received_events_url': 'https://api.github.com/users/raoanirudh/received_events', 'type': 'User', 'site_admin': False}]",[],micheles,2463856.0,User,False,[],https://github.com/gem/oq-engine/pull/3127.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/gem/oq-engine/pulls/3127/commits,380,"['63321810f1d18a5d12bba01b8748f2dd459a903f', '1ffb710061150373868ed598887ffb7d6a382206', '26745ead3b14c2b64080176be3dc4b3135781360', '25c5c73e8d35ceb272bc5db6c75659d23a2622d3', '0b54a0499dcc06c4cc6b37f1b0a71d9dc7b255d8', '76738fae4ce01706e33b6f82081ef8d8054f97c7', '26bcd3662591dc76711c8a453a785ebbcac48779', '80246fc518d75ffe6f3557804073c05e92241f0c', 'ba9d7225c74c94023146ed60e6fee7b7309aff37', 'a243943b1b725253e8645671419b6a278e180ce9', '6360f6b33cab4f7f96a912ac15d5fb859367df8e', 'c3f17490f4f7d3669542d35aabd1649d7d60639c', '87b337f2997f35c70f8770992b1e8f784d02f0d3']"

> publishing developmental releases of pre-releases to general purpose
> public index servers is strongly discouraged, as it makes the version
> identifier difficult to parse for human readers. If such a release
> needs to be published, it is substantially clearer to instead create a
> new pre-release by incrementing the numeric component.

6937,PullRequestEvent,2018-03-17 21:40:11+00:00,51397392,pytroll/satpy,1828519,davidh-ssec,13004956.0,pytroll,175706296,,Fix bug in dep tree when modifier deps are modified wavelengths,closed,False,229,"This PR fixes a bug I encountered when trying to add rayleigh correction to the ABI natural composite. The default rayleigh corrector/modifier in `visir.yaml` depends on a `sunz_corrected` 0.67 band. This is C02 for ABI. C02 is also needed to make the natural RGB and is specified by name.

Before this PR these dependencies would show up in the dependency tree as two separate nodes. One as the 0.67 modified key, one as the C02 modified key. From what I can tell this happens because when the dep tree checks if C02 is in the dep tree it thinks it isn't there, but it is actually there as the 0.67 dependency. The key for the 0.67 dependency is never ""filled in"" with the details of it's unmodified dataset (name, resolution, calibration, etc). This PR fixes this. I think I had this behavior implemented in the past but removed it for some reason. Tests pass, so I don't know.
This bug comes up semi-randomly based on the order that dependencies are loaded/processed.

P.S. I thought I'd start making PRs from my own fork instead of the satpy one. Maintainers should still have push permissions to my branch through this PR. This follows how I've seen other projects do it where they only have a master branch instead of a develop/master branch pair.

6996,PullRequestEvent,2015-10-21 14:05:49+00:00,28043871,edoburu/django-parler-rest,1199046,AdrianLC,738973.0,edoburu,48335526,,"Validate empty translations (i.e. {}), minor fixes, test improvements",open,False,10,"Hello,
Sorry this should probably be in separate pull requests but I had some bug fixes directly on my fork and could not sync with upstream until today.

* Current implementation accepts empty translations as valid. I.e. `'translations': {}` and so we encountered objects without translated texts in any language, which in our case and in most I supposed are pretty much useless.
I added an `allow_empty` option similar to the one in RelatedFields to ``TranslatedFieldsField`. This is disabled by default but can be explicitly declared to maintain the previous behaviour.

* Insert ``.validated_data`` in the translations ``to_internal_value`` instead of directly ``.data``. I honestly do not remember the exact reason to this but I'm certain it had something to do with nested relations and a nested ``SlugRelatedField`` not being restored. Anyway the parent serializers always expect ``validated_data`` to save the children objects as needed.
* Some tests were broken because of translation caching and the pk being always the same due to the automatic database rollback.
I leave the version numbering to you. 1.4 probably?

We've being using this in production for months now and I have to use it in another project where Github dependencies are not allowed so if you could please, please release to PyPI as soon as possible... it would be really helpful to us. I will have to copy the whole directory in the meantime as I absolutely don't want to release a fork to PyPI.

There's a hysterical explanation: Originally, the ZEO protocol was
synchronous and mirrored the storage API. That was too slow, so store
was made asynchronous.  But then there was no way to return new
serials, so we added a serialnos client API that the server called
during TPC. This was used both to serials and errors.  Later, tpc_vote
was added, which is a synchronous method. That would have been an
execllent opportunity to fix this, but noooooooooo.
I'd like the server vote logic to get simpler, and we also want to get rid
of serialnos, as it makes it hard to commit multiple transactions at
once on the client.  We can't get rid of serialnos now, because the
client needs to work with old servers.

client saw an unhandled conflict error, it would invalidate it's cache
for that object, allowing it to eventually recover when caches had
missed invalidations. This required a change to ClientStorage, which
meant that the server wouldn't work with older ZEO versions, which

7442,PullRequestEvent,2016-11-20 17:51:29+00:00,3510804,GalSim-developers/GalSim,623887,rmjarvis,1462196.0,GalSim-developers,94526023,,#820 Make it a bit easier to run config functions without going through galsim executable,open,False,838,"@esheldon reported on issue #820 that when running config functions by hand within python, e.g. `galsim.config.BuildGSObject(config, 'gal')`, it wasn't allowing the rng to be automatic for things that have `_takes_rng = True`.

This is normally not a problem when running config files through the `galsim` executable, since prior to that call, an appropriate rng will have bee constructed.  However, I think it does make sense to allow users to call functions such as this without requiring them to manually make the rng and put it in `config['rng']`.  So that's done here.

However, I also took this opportunity to go through the missing config coverage (which was a lot) and add unit tests to cover the things that weren't being covered by anything else yet.  Along the way, I found and fixed a number of bugs in edge case scenarios and made a few other changes to make it easier to call the config functions manually.  Here is a complete list of changes:
- Setting config['rng'] is no longer required when manually running commands like `galsim.config.BuildGSObject`.
- Changed the name of galsim.config.CalculateNoiseVar to the slightly more verbose, but also more readable CalculateNoiseVariance.
- Allow PoissonNoise without any sky level, in which case only the shot noise in the signal photons contribute to the noise.  Likewise for CCDNoise.
- Let 'None' in the config file mean `None`.
- Require 'max_extra_noise' to be explicitly set for photon shooting if you want it rather than have it default to 0.01.
- Added `--except_abor`t option to `galsim` executable to abort execution if a file has an error, rather than just reporting the exception and continuing on (which is still the default behavior).
- Added optional probability parameter 'p' for Random bool values.

- Allow `drawImage` to take a non-uniform WCS when default constructing the image from either `nx`,`ny` or `bounds`.  (Note: this one is not a config change -- it was just a restriction that we had in `drawImage` that isn't actually necessary.)
Also a couple other functionality-neutral changes you'll notice here:

- When people used galsim.utilities for things, there was a mix of using the full `galsim.utilities.blah` vs `from . import utilities` ... `utilities.blah`.  It seemed that the former was more common, so I switched everything to use that style for consistency.

- Josh pointed out during the hack week that `_absdet` in SBTransform` wasn't actually correct.  It's really the total flux scaling.  And there was another parameter called `_fluxScaling`, which is really the amplitude scaling.  So I renamed these `_fluxScaling` and `_ampScaling` respectively to be clearer.  And I kept `_absdet` to actually mean |det(m)|.

7803,PullRequestEvent,2015-11-07 18:13:58+00:00,8463294,mozilla/treeherder,501702,edmorley,131524.0,mozilla,50041695,,Bug 1222718 - Enable non-default eslint rules that already pass,open,False,1130,"The following rules are already passing, without any code changes on our side. I've reduced the list down to just the rules that I think we want to enforce. The fact that we currently pass them means that these rules generally aren't too contentious, however I'm happy to adjust as needed, either now or when we first hit failures in them.
enforce newline at the end of file, with no multiple empty lines
make sure for-in loops have an if statement
require a capital letter for constructors
disallow the omission of parentheses when invoking a constructor with no arguments
disallow use of the Array constructor
disallow use of arguments.caller or arguments.callee
disallow division operators explicitly at beginning of regular expression
disallow use of labels for anything other than loops and switches
disallow use of empty destructuring patterns
disallow the use of leading or trailing decimal points in numeric literals
disallow labels that share a name with a variable
disallow use of the new operator when not part of an assignment or comparison
disallow use of new operator for Function object
disallow the use of the Object constructor
disallows creating new instances of String,Number, and Boolean
disallow use of octal escape sequences in string literals, such as var foo = ""Copyright \251""
disallow use of assignment in return statement
disallow comparisons where both sides are exactly the same
disallow use of the comma operator
disallow shadowing of names such as arguments
disallow space between function identifier and application
disallow trailing whitespace at the end of lines
disallow use of undefined when initializing variables
Avoid code that looks like two expressions but is actually one
disallow usage of expressions in statement position
disallow use of the void operator
disallow use of the with statement
require or disallow use of semicolons instead of ASI
require a space after return, throw, and case
controls location of Use Strict Directives

7832,PullRequestEvent,2015-06-30 13:12:31+00:00,8463294,mozilla/treeherder,501702,edmorley,131524.0,mozilla,38837053,,Bug 1178232 - Remove support for multiple Datasources of the same type,closed,False,693,The datasource table has a 'dataset' field, to allow for multiple datasources of the same type (for partitioning
column2: eg the ""1"" in `mozilla-central_jobs_1`). However we have never used it, so let's just remove it.

column1: Unfortunately the tests are failing during the `init_master_db` call, since it is trying to apply the migrations on top of the SQL in `treeherder.sql.tmpl`, which now no longer contains the field in question.
I guess this stems from our weird use of a combination of both raw SQL for DB init, plus DB migrations (in the form of 0001_initial.py).
@maurodoglio what do you suggest to resolve this? I could:
* Leave `treeherder.sql.tmpl` unchanged (so the migrations apply cleanly on top of it), but this seems suboptimal since it now no longer reflects the desired end state of the schema.
* Or I could remove the `call_command(""migrate"")` here:
https://github.com/mozilla/treeherder/blob/master/treeherder/model/management/commands/init_master_db.py#L74 (but then I'm presuming things would still break if migrate was run manually later?)
* Or we could get rid of the reference data SQL file and only use the Django model representation of it?

The latter seems like the cleanest solution perhaps? (And given https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1178225#c2 it seems like we need to clean up models.py since some things are missing there compared to the SQL).

8237,PullRequestEvent,2018-05-02 15:52:37+00:00,71932349,ray-project/ray,11225374,eric-jj,22125274.0,ray-project,185351424,,Improve shared_ptr usage,closed,False,1981,"The PR will resolve two issues.

1. Use the make_shared to replace std::shared_ptr(new XXX), make_shared will combine the reference count and object create(new) to a memory allocation, but the std::shared_ptr(new XXX) will cause two allocations.

2. Pass the shared_ptr by reference in some cases instead of pass by value. Copy of a shared_ptr is not cheap, which will call the interlock increase and decrease during the function call. Interlock operation will lock the data bus which means all the cores will be paused to wait for it. In my previous project, when we disable the perf counters(which use the interlock operation internal), we almost get 40% latency improvement. ",https://api.github.com/repos/ray-project/ray/issues/1981,,2018-05-02T15:52:37Z,2018-05-02T15:52:37Z,11,221,249,25,CONTRIBUTOR,[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/ray-project/ray/pull/1981.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/ray-project/ray/pulls/1981/commits,380,"['7f98fb4b73e0070fd80af6d48c84dc81fabc20d4', 'f1309e1c7e257a5f262ce4333c0f45ba133b805b', '2763900c3bffb070cdae898809c313d4d2f60007', '26919f0f1a7c108f6aa2e60b17b916fbba03e338', '2324da9cd721e2a3f13c68fc6ac8e6cf3219557f', '5e7448e34eee863de6384beaf859ea1647ebd3dc', 'd673b703bb6aa4aad0a3c565b6dcbb108e7e3517', 'e87393f36974c0c446e347150cc0bc61000e9c6b', '1ad4a5ec8bc141622a4f7edcd42ece213c4be481', '3c4f99c3ee20e857f2d1bf867c7970db082017f9', 'edb92a431d9ba6de9f843fe6b68e008a896b05de', '9004232f4b7b682557781f0045af55de41697a22', '429be3c91eb00fb78e6dccb042e99d51376ee31c']"

8408,PullRequestEvent,2016-06-01 13:34:29+00:00,21987374,django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api,7396393,scottfisk,11215076.0,django-json-api,72070672,,Fix return of stale data on PATCH of to-one relationship,closed,False,247,"I noticed that PATCH requests made to relationship views with to-one relationships correctly update the underlying relationship with the passed data, but the response I get back is stale (it returns the original relationship instead of reflecting the new one).

This is only a problem with to-one relationships, but not to-many, as that code patch updates the `related_instance_or_manager` directly, while in the case of a to-one relationship it is returning a serialized version of the relationship it fetches in the beginning of the function.

I have fixed this by refreshing the `related_instance_or_manager` at the end of the block that updates to-one relationships. I have additionally added asserts to the methods that test updating to-one and to-many relationships to make sure that the response data is equal to the data passed in through the request.
As an aside, I'm not entirely sure if these updates should return a 200 (as they do now) or a 204. The spec has the following to say about the response codes when updating relationships:
A server MUST return a 204 No Content status code if an update is successful and the representation of the resource in the request matches the result.

If a server accepts an update but also changes the targeted relationship(s) in other ways than those specified by the request, it MUST return a 200 OK response. The response document MUST include a representation of the updated relationship(s).

My reading is that maybe this should be a 204 response as the new relationship is exactly the one that has been passed in through the PATCH request, and nothing else about the relationship has changed. That said, regardless of whether it should be a 204 or 200, it shouldn't be returning stale relationship data which is what this PR aims to fix.",https://api.github.com/repos/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/issues/247,2016-06-01T13:34:28Z,2016-06-01T13:34:28Z,2016-06-01T13:34:29Z,2,3,0,2,[],[],[],[],scottfisk,7396393.0,User,False,[],https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/pull/247.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/pulls/247/commits,380,[]
